## 1. Role Justification

The Gameplay Engineer #1 is a mid-level role focused on gameplay scripting, feature implementation, debugging, and version control best practices. This is a P1 hire required for Stage 5 (Full Production). The role sits under Dmitri Volkov's direction, implementing gameplay features assigned by senior engineers, writing unit tests, fixing bugs, and participating in code reviews.

This hire provides additional implementation capacity alongside senior gameplay engineers, enabling parallel feature development while maintaining code quality through established testing practices.

**Pipeline Stages Owned:** 3 (Gameplay Feature Implementation), 5 (Feature Development), 6 (Code Review Participation)

# Position Specification Document

**Role:** Gameplay Engineer #1 (G16)
**Role Family:** Engineering — Gameplay
**Seniority:** Mid-Level (L1)
**Team:** Casual Games Studio — Engineering Division
**Reports To:** Dmitri Volkov (Senior Game Engineer)
**Priority:** P1
**Compensation Band:** $90K–$120K base
**Document ID:** PSD-2026-G16-001
**Generated:** 2026-04-10T08:00:00Z
**Generated By:** CHRO Office (Automated Pipeline)
**Recruitment Phase:** Phase 3A — Engineering Ramp

---

## 1. Role Justification

The Gameplay Engineer #1 is a mid-level role focused on gameplay scripting, feature implementation, debugging, and version control best practices. This is a P1 hire required for Stage 5 (Full Production). The role sits under Dmitri Volkov's direction, implementing gameplay features assigned by senior engineers, writing unit tests, fixing bugs, and participating in code reviews.

This hire provides additional implementation capacity alongside senior gameplay engineers, enabling parallel feature development while maintaining code quality through established testing practices.

**Pipeline Stages Owned:** 3 (Gameplay Feature Implementation), 5 (Feature Development), 6 (Code Review Participation)

---

## 2. Required Competencies (weighted)

| Competency                    | Weight | Minimum Threshold | Assessment Method                  |
| ----------------------------- | ------ | ----------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| Gameplay Scripting (C#/Unity) | 30%    | ≥ 3.5/5           | Coding challenge                   |
| Feature Implementation        | 25%    | ≥ 3.5/5           | Feature development exercise       |
| Debugging & Testing           | 20%    | ≥ 3.5/5           | Debugging challenge + test writing |
| Version Control (Git)         | 15%    | ≥ 3.5/5           | Git workflow exercise              |
| Team Collaboration            | 10%    | ≥ 3/5             | Behavioral interview               |

---

## 3. Must-Have Qualifications

- 2+ years gameplay programming experience
- C# scripting for Unity; implemented gameplay mechanics in shipped titles
- End-to-end feature delivery from spec to ship
- Debugging and unit testing practices
- Git workflow proficiency with collaborative PR process

---

## 4. Nice-to-Have Qualifications

- Shipped live-ops titles with 10M+ players
- Experience with tournament matchmaking systems
- Published indie game titles on Steam or mobile platforms

---

## 5. Compensation Structure

| Component         | Range / Value                               |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| Base Salary       | $90K–$120K (target: $105K, 50th percentile) |
| Performance Bonus | Up to 8% of base (target: 6%)               |
| Equity            | 0.04%–0.08% (Mid-Level tier)                |
| Signing Bonus     | Up to $8K                                   |
| Relocation        | Up to $5K                                   |
| Conference Budget | $3K/year                                    |

---

## 6. Success Criteria (30/60/90 Day Plan)

| Milestone   | Criteria                                                               |
| ----------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Day 1**   | Engine access granted, dev environment running, repo access configured |
| **Week 1**  | First PR merged (minor bug fix); introduced to Dmitri Volkov and team  |
| **Month 1** | First gameplay feature implemented end-to-end; unit tests passing      |
| **Month 2** | Multiple features delivered; bug fix acceptance rate > 90%             |
| **Month 3** | Active contributor to gameplay codebase; participates in code reviews  |

---
